name: Michigan Pharmacy Technician Registration
description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Michigan - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Michigan Board of Pharmacy
provider.parentOrganization: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA)

address.description:
Bureau of Professional Licensing
P.O. Box 30670
Lansing, MI 48909

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Michigan pharmacy technician license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Michigan lists a $137.70 pharmacy technician application and initial licensure fee, with a $61.20 renewal fee for the two-year cycle.
Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Michigan have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Michigan. To work in Michigan, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Michigan?

acceptedAnswer.text: Michigan’s current licensing report says pharmacy technician applications average about 90 days from receipt to issuance.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Michigan while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contact the Michigan Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Michigan.
